Steps to Perform: 1. Go to /projects/{id}/editor for a project that has a generated page
What Should Happen: Editor loads with three panels: left sidebar (Sections), center canvas (page preview), right sidebar (Inspector). No errors shown.

Test Case: Editor shows all generated sections on the canvas
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor for a project with multiple sections
What Should Happen: All sections appear vertically stacked on the center canvas, matching the generated content

Test Case: Editor redirects if no page exists
Steps to Perform: 1. Go to /projects/{id}/editor for a project with no generated page
What Should Happen: Redirects to the builder page (/projects/{id}/builder)

Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor
What Should Happen: Top toolbar shows version number (e.g., 'v1')

Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor
What Should Happen: Top toolbar shows 'Editor' label on the left side

Test Case: Save button is disabled when nothing changed
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor without making any changes
What Should Happen: 'Save' button appears grayed out / not clickable

Test Case: Save button becomes active after a change
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor 2. Change any text or setting
What Should Happen: 'Save' button turns blue and becomes clickable. An 'Unsaved' yellow badge appears.

Steps to Perform: 1. Make a change 2. Click 'Save'
What Should Happen: Button text changes to 'Saving...' briefly, then back to 'Save'. The 'Unsaved' badge disappears.

Test Case: Changes persist after save and refresh
Steps to Perform: 1. Change a heading text 2. Click Save 3. Refresh the browser
What Should Happen: The changed heading still shows the new text

Test Case: Preview button switches to preview mode
Steps to Perform: 1. Click the 'Preview' button
What Should Happen: Left sidebar and right sidebar disappear. Canvas shows the page as it would look to a visitor. Button text changes to 'Edit'.

Test Case: Edit button switches back to edit mode
Steps to Perform: 1. Click 'Preview' to enter preview mode 2. Click 'Edit'
What Should Happen: Left and right sidebars reappear. Canvas shows editing controls again.

Steps to Perform: 1. Enter Preview mode 2. Click the Desktop icon
What Should Happen: Canvas takes full width to show desktop layout

Steps to Perform: 1. Enter Preview mode 2. Click the Tablet icon
What Should Happen: Canvas shrinks to approximately tablet width (768px). Content reflows for tablet.

Steps to Perform: 1. Enter Preview mode 2. Click the Mobile icon
What Should Happen: Canvas shrinks to approximately phone width (375px). Content reflows for mobile.

Test Case: Preview mode selector only visible in preview
Steps to Perform: 1. Open editor in normal Edit mode
What Should Happen: Desktop/Tablet/Mobile buttons are NOT visible. They only appear after clicking 'Preview'.

Steps to Perform: 1. Click the 'Live' button
What Should Happen: A new browser tab opens showing the published version of the page

Steps to Perform: 1. Look at the left sidebar
What Should Happen: All page sections are listed with their type names (e.g., Hero, Features, FAQ) and index numbers (1, 2, 3...)

Steps to Perform: 1. Click on a section name in the left sidebar
What Should Happen: The section gets highlighted in the sidebar. The canvas scrolls to that section. The right sidebar shows that section's properties.

Test Case: Selected section is highlighted on the canvas
Steps to Perform: 1. Click a section in the sidebar
What Should Happen: The section on the canvas gets a blue outline/border to show it is selected

What Should Happen: The variant name (e.g., 'split-image', 'cards') appears in gray text below the section type

Steps to Perform: 1. Click the '+' button at the top of the Sections panel

What Should Happen: A list of variants for that type appears (e.g., centered, split-image, background-image, offer-focused). A back button appears to go back to the type list.

What Should Happen: The new section appears on the canvas. It also appears in the section list.

What Should Happen: The add section menu opens. The new section is inserted between those two sections.

Steps to Perform: 1. Hover over the first section in the list

Steps to Perform: 1. Hover over a section 2. Click the eye icon

What Should Happen: The eye icon changes back to an open eye. The section reappears at full opacity. The section shows again on the canvas.

Steps to Perform: 1. Start deleting a section 2. Click 'Cancel' on the confirmation dialog
What Should Happen: The section remains unchanged

What Should Happen: Text content (headings, descriptions, etc.) matches what is set in the inspector

Steps to Perform: 1. Click on a section on the canvas

Steps to Perform: 1. Switch to Preview mode 2. Hover over a section

Test Case: Click on text to edit it
